controls.label: Does your business have an Alternative Identity Proofing process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have an Alternative Identity Proofing process in place. You must establish a documented Alternative Identity Proofing process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Do you have risk-based systems and controls in place to use alternative identity proofing processes when customers cannot provide standard identification evidence?
controls.detail-label: Describe your risk-based systems and controls for alternative identity proofing
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure does not implement risk-based systems and controls to support alternative identity proofing when customers cannot obtain standard identification evidence. Establish documented risk-based systems and controls that allow your organisation to use alternative identity proofing processes when customers are unable to provide the necessary information or evidence of identity under standard requirements, as required under rule 4.15.1.

controls.label: Do you have a procedure to accept self-attestation from customers when alternative identity proofing processes cannot establish identity?
controls.detail-label: Describe your self-attestation procedure and how you implement it
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure does not include a self-attestation mechanism for cases where alternative identity proofing processes cannot establish customer identity. Establish a documented procedure to accept self-attestation from customers certifying that the identity information they have provided is true and correct, to be used only when alternative identity proofing under rule 4.15.1 or 4.15.1A cannot establish identity, as required under rule 4.15.2.

controls.label: Do you apply appropriate levels of ongoing customer due diligence to identify, mitigate and manage ML/TF risk when customer identities have been established using self-attestation?
controls.detail-label: Describe your ongoing customer due diligence procedures for self-attested identities
controls.remediation: Your Ongoing Customer Due Diligence process does not account for identities established via self-attestation. Implement appropriate levels of ongoing due diligence proportionate to the ML/TF risks associated with customers whose identities were established using self-attestation, including monitoring and review procedures to identify and mitigate risk, as required under rule 4.15.3.

controls.label: Do you have controls to prevent reliance on self-attestation when you know or have reason to believe it is incorrect or misleading?
controls.detail-label: Describe your validation and verification controls for self-attestation
controls.remediation: Your identity verification procedure does not include safeguards to reject self-attestation when it is or appears to be incorrect or misleading. Establish controls and procedures to validate self-attested information and ensure you do not rely on any self-attestation that you know or have reason to believe is incorrect or misleading, as required under rule 4.15.4.

controls.label: Do you have a procedure to rely on copies of identification documents (rather than originals or certified copies) during COVID-19 Pandemic measures, in accordance with your risk-based systems and controls?
controls.detail-label: Describe your procedure for accepting document copies during COVID-19 circumstances
controls.remediation: Your identity verification procedure does not accommodate acceptance of document copies when COVID-19 Pandemic measures prevent verification of originals or certified copies. Establish a risk-based procedure to accept copies of identification documents in lieu of originals or certified copies/extracts when COVID-19 Pandemic measures prevent access to the original documents, as required under rule 4.15.5.

controls.label: Do you understand and correctly apply the definition of 'COVID-19 Pandemic measures' as measures implemented or recommended by Australian government bodies, or reasonable measures adopted by your organisation or persons you deal with?
controls.remediation: Your alternative identity proofing procedures do not correctly apply the regulatory definition of 'COVID-19 Pandemic measures'. Ensure your procedures correctly recognise and respond to measures implemented or recommended by Australian government bodies, as well as reasonable measures adopted by your organisation or persons you interact with (including those in foreign jurisdictions), when determining whether alternative identity proofing is permissible under rules 4.15.6(1), 4.15.6(2), and 4.15.6(3).

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: General Alternative Identity Proofing Requirement
groups.description: Establish risk-based systems and controls to support alternative identity proofing when customers cannot provide standard identification evidence.

groups.title: Alternative Processes for COVID-19 Circumstances
groups.description: Implement alternative identity proofing procedures (such as acceptance of multiple secondary documents) to address cases where customers possess but cannot produce identification due to COVID-19 Pandemic measures.

groups.title: Self-Attestation Acceptance
groups.description: Establish procedures to accept customer self-attestation certifying the truth and correctness of identity information when alternative identity proofing processes cannot establish identity.

groups.title: Ongoing Due Diligence for Self-Attested Identities
groups.description: Apply appropriate levels of ongoing customer due diligence to identify, mitigate and manage ML/TF risks associated with identities established via self-attestation.

groups.title: Self-Attestation Validation and Rejection
groups.description: Implement controls to prevent reliance on self-attestation when you know or have reason to believe it is incorrect or misleading.

groups.title: Document Copy Acceptance During COVID-19
groups.description: Establish procedures to accept copies of identification documents in lieu of originals or certified copies when COVID-19 Pandemic measures prevent access to original documents.

groups.title: Understanding COVID-19 Pandemic Measures
groups.description: Ensure your procedures correctly apply the regulatory definition of 'COVID-19 Pandemic measures' to determine when alternative identity proofing and verification is permissible.
